qa_pairs.question: How do I add a person who already has a Globus account to a shared collection?
qa_pairs.answer: If someone already has a Globus account, you can add them to a shared collection by entering their name or Globus username, selecting the appropriate match from the list, and then clicking 'Use Selected'. This method does not require any action on their part.

qa_pairs.question: What methods are available for sharing a collection with a group of people?
qa_pairs.answer: You can share with a number of people simultaneously by using a group, searching by group name or UUID.

qa_pairs.question: How can I avoid ambiguity when searching for a group name to share with?
qa_pairs.answer: Group names may be ambiguous, so to avoid this, it is recommended to use the group’s UUID, which is available on the Groups page.

qa_pairs.question: How do I enable write permissions for users accessing a shared collection?
qa_pairs.answer: To enable write permissions, click on the 'write' checkbox in the form. In the access list, it is also possible to add or remove the write permissions by clicking the checkbox next to the name under the 'WRITE' column.

qa_pairs.question: Can read access be removed from a shared collection?
qa_pairs.answer: No, it is not possible to remove read access from a shared collection.

qa_pairs.question: How do I remove users or groups from the access list of a shared collection?
qa_pairs.answer: Deleting users or groups from the list of people you are sharing with is as simple as clicking the ‘x’ at the end of the line containing their information.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to remove a shared collection?
qa_pairs.answer: To remove a shared collection, click on 'Collections' on the left side of the screen, then click on the 'Shareable by You' tab, click on the title of the Shared Collection you want to remove, click on the 'Delete Collection' button on the right side of the screen, and finally, confirm deleting it by clicking on the red button.

qa_pairs.question: Will my files be affected if I remove a shared collection?
qa_pairs.answer: No, your files will not be affected by removing a shared collection, nor will those others may have uploaded.

qa_pairs.question: What security considerations should I keep in mind when sharing files using Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: When sharing files, ensure you have permission to share if you're not the owner, verify you are sharing with only those you intend to (email is recommended unless you have the exact account name), trust the owner of any group you share with, back up important files if granting write access, and restrict sharing to a subdirectory rather than your top-level home directory.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary benefit of using Globus groups?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus groups provide an easy way to manage permissions for sharing with multiple users.

qa_pairs.question: How do I create a new Globus group?
qa_pairs.answer: To create a group, click on the 'Groups' button on the left sidebar, click 'Create New Group', enter the group name and description, select if it's visible to only group members or all Globus users, and then click 'Create Group'.

qa_pairs.question: How do I invite users to a Globus group?
qa_pairs.answer: Once a group has been created, users can be added by selecting 'Invite users', then either entering an email address (preferred) or searching for the username. After selection, click the 'Add' button, and they will receive an email invitation to join.

qa_pairs.question: How can I change a user's role or status within a Globus group?
qa_pairs.answer: Click on a user within the group to modify their membership. You can change their role and status, and then click 'Save Changes' to commit the changes.

qa_pairs.question: What roles can be assigned to users in a Globus group, and what do they allow?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can be assigned roles including Admin, which grants full access; Manager, which allows changing user roles; or Member, which has no management functions.
